title: cota tinctoria subsp. tinctoria
intro: Cota tinctoria subsp. tinctoria, also known as dyer's chamomile or dyer's knapweed, is a subspecies of the annual plant Cota tinctoria that belongs to the dandelion family, Asteraceae. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever. The health benefits of Cota tinctoria subsp. tinctoria include relief from menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
